parsed.mechanism: Expert-choice routing dynamically routes tokens to each attention head (from A), and this induced sparse, head-specialized attention structure is used to precondition the optimizer's per-parameter updates so that it produces faster, more stable convergence (à la B's accelerated optimization).
parsed.transfer: The expert-choice token-routing mechanism from A transfers to B as a structural prior that an optimizer exploits to accelerate convergence.
parsed.open_problem: Can MoSA-style expert-choice attention routing be coupled with a Conda-style optimizer to causally produce 2-2.5x faster convergence on LLaMA-scale models?
parsed.niche_name: Routing-Aware Optimizers for Sparse Expert-Choice Attention
parsed.primary_quote: Conda achieves 2-2.5x faster convergence than AdamW on LLaMA series
